title: Add queryStream() generator/Embind bridge after tracer bullet
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- Follow-on work from openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md

## What to build
Implement the streaming query slice by adding `queryStream()` and the `std::generator<Hit>` / Embind async iterator bridge, including correct cleanup when iteration ends early.

**Context:** This follow-on isolates the riskiest generator/Embind seam until after the basic query path works.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md "Follow-on Work"

**Files / Systems:**
- `src/core/` query generator surface
- `src/bind/` async iterator bridge
- `js/tragar.ts`
- streaming lifecycle tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `queryStream()` yields hits in score order through an async iterator surface
- [ ] Early `break` / iterator `return()` releases underlying C++ generator resources without leaks
- [ ] The streaming path preserves typed error behavior for failures during query execution
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
- tRAG-32j

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.

title: Add OPFS/IndexedDB persistence slice after tracer bullet
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- Follow-on work from openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md

## What to build
Implement the persistence slice for tRAGar by introducing the OPFS / IndexedDB store path on top of the proven in-memory retrieval flow.

**Context:** This follow-on validates namespace persistence, reopen/query behavior, and store fallback after the no-network bootstrap path is already known-good.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md "Follow-on Work"

**Files / Systems:**
- `src/platform/` store bindings
- `js/seams/` store implementations
- `js/tragar.ts`
- persistence and fallback test harness

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A supported browser can ingest, close, recreate, and query using persisted corpus data
- [ ] OPFS unavailability triggers IndexedDB fallback with the documented warning and `storeMode` value
- [ ] Persistence behavior matches the public-api and browser-compat specs for the implemented slice
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
- tRAG-32j

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.

title: Integrate transformers.js default embedder after tracer bullet
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- Follow-on work from openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md

## What to build
Implement the first post-bootstrap default-path slice: integrate the transformers.js embedder and its lazy-loading/model-loading semantics into the existing vertical slice.

**Context:** This is the first follow-on after the deterministic tracer bullet proves the architecture. It validates the real default embedder boundary while keeping persistence and streaming out of scope.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/design.md "Follow-on Work"

**Files / Systems:**
- `js/seams/` transformers embedder implementation
- `js/tragar.ts`
- browser/network loading test harness
- relevant OpenSpec-aligned tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The default embedder path lazy-loads transformers.js on first embed/query use rather than on `create()`
- [ ] Successful ingest/query works with the default embedder path in a supported browser environment
- [ ] Failures during model/module load are surfaced as typed embedder errors
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
- tRAG-32j

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
close_reason: Implemented: lazy-loading transformers.js seam, typed EmbedderLoadFailed error, 14 new tests (46 total green)

title: Shipped browser tracer-bullet example and smoke test
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- OpenSpec: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/specs/tracer-bullet-demo/spec.md

## What to build
Ship a small browser-hosted tracer-bullet example and smoke test that exercise the deterministic bootstrap slice end-to-end in one page load. The example should load the local build, ingest a tiny fixture corpus, run one query, and display or assert the result.

**Context:** This is Slice 3 from the tracer-bullet plan and makes the vertical slice demoable and independently verifiable.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/tasks.md §2.3, §3.3; proposal.md; design.md

**Files / Systems:**
- `examples/tracer-bullet/`
- browser test/smoke harness
- build output wiring for local browser execution
- minimal docs/README note for the example

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A developer can open or serve the tracer-bullet example and observe one successful `create() -> ingest() -> query()` cycle
- [ ] The example uses the deterministic custom embedder path and performs no external model downloads
- [ ] A smoke test covers the example in a supported browser
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
- tRAG-32j

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.

title: Deterministic in-memory ingest/query tracer-bullet slice
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- OpenSpec: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/{proposal.md,tasks.md,design.md}

## What to build
Implement the first true retrieval slice for tRAGar on top of the bootstrap skeleton: `create() -> ingest() -> query()` using `TRAGar.stores.memory()` and a deterministic `TRAGar.embedders.custom(...)` implementation. This slice proves chunk -> embed -> store -> search end-to-end without network or persistence dependencies.

**Context:** This is Slice 2 from the tracer-bullet plan and the first narrow capability that delivers actual retrieval behavior.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/tasks.md §2.1-2.2, §3.2; proposal.md; design.md

**Files / Systems:**
- `src/core/` chunk/embed/store/search path
- `src/bind/` query/ingest bridge
- `js/tragar.ts`
- `js/seams/` or equivalent seam bootstrap location
- test harness for deterministic ingest/query behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `TRAGar.create({ store: TRAGar.stores.memory(), embedder: TRAGar.embedders.custom(...) })` supports one successful ingest/query cycle
- [ ] The deterministic embedder path performs no network access and needs no persistent browser storage
- [ ] `query()` returns stable, testable hits for a fixed fixture corpus under the deterministic embedder
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
- tRAG-5ip

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
close_reason: Implemented pure-JS deterministic ingest/query slice: embedders.custom(), paragraph chunker, L2-normalize + dot-product search, stats(). 32/32 tests pass (22 new Slice 2 + 10 Slice 1).

title: Bootstrap tracer-bullet library skeleton with create()/close() and memory mode
description:
## Parent
- tRAG-cvr — Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
- OpenSpec: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/{proposal.md,tasks.md,design.md}

## What to build
Implement the first executable bootstrap slice for tRAGar: a minimal library skeleton that can construct and close a TRAGar instance using the in-memory store path, proving the TS wrapper ↔ Embind ↔ C++ bootstrap path without yet taking on ingest/query semantics.

**Context:** This is Slice 1 from the tracer-bullet plan. It exists to establish the repo skeleton and lifecycle boundary before retrieval behavior is added.
Ref: openspec/changes/add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice/tasks.md §3.1; proposal.md; design.md

**Files / Systems:**
- `CMakeLists.txt`, `CMakePresets.json`
- `src/core/` bootstrap surface
- `src/bind/` Embind glue
- `js/tragar.ts`
- `js/types.ts`
- build/test harness for native + wasm bootstrap

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A minimal `TRAGar.create({ store: TRAGar.stores.memory() })` path exists and returns an instance with `storeMode === 'memory'`
- [ ] `close()` succeeds and subsequent method calls reject with the appropriate unavailable/store-closed error for the implemented surface
- [ ] Native and/or wasm tests cover bootstrap creation and close lifecycle behavior
- [ ] Automated tests cover the slice end-to-end

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met: create()/close() lifecycle implemented, storeMode='memory' works, namespace validated, 10 TS tests pass, 8 C++ doctest assertions pass. Build system scaffolded (CMake + presets). Embind bridge skeleton in src/bind/.

title: Plan tracer-bullet vertical slice for tRAGar
description:
Why: The repository has strong v0.1 and seam-expansion specs but no executable baseline slice proving the build, WASM boundary, JS API, and ingest/query loop work together.

Scope:
- Define a tracer-bullet vertical slice that is minimal/dev-first
- Update OpenSpec artifacts for the tracer bullet
- Produce a phased implementation plan for the first code-bearing milestones

Non-goals:
- Do not absorb the 8 existing seam-expansion proposals
- Do not require OPFS/IndexedDB, transformers.js, or queryStream in the first slice

Deliverables:
- OpenSpec change `add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice`
- WAI research, design, and plan artifacts under `tragar-tracer-bullet`
- Recommended slice ordering and follow-on implementation tickets

Outcome: a concrete, bounded proposal and plan that de-risks the first implementation work without widening scope.
design: Chosen direction: minimal/dev-first tracer bullet. Canonical bootstrap path is TRAGar.create({ store: TRAGar.stores.memory(), embedder: TRAGar.embedders.custom(...) }) plus ingest() and query() in a shipped browser example. queryStream, real persistence, and transformers.js are follow-on slices after the C++/Embind/TS seam is proven.
acceptance_criteria:
- OpenSpec change `add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice` is scaffolded and passes `openspec validate add-tracer-bullet-vertical-slice --strict`
- WAI research, design, and plan artifacts document scope, architecture boundaries, and implementation sequencing for the tracer bullet
- Issue description, notes, and design explicitly define tracer-bullet scope, non-goals, deliverables, and relationship to the 8 existing seam-expansion OpenSpec changes
notes: Related OpenSpec baseline review: tracer bullet is intentionally limited to the current v0.1 baseline and must not overlap with add-neighbor-graph, add-projector-seam, add-clusterer-seam, add-file-store, add-vector-access-export, add-intrinsic-dim-stats, add-contrastive-queries, or add-vocabulary-chunker.

title: Decide: default embedder model — English-only vs multilingual
description:
## Why this decision exists

tRAGar's target use case includes Spanish-language corpora (the dogfood demo, the companion essay). The v0.1 default is `Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (English-trained, ~23 MB, fast). The multilingual alternative is `Xenova/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2` (~120 MB, better Spanish recall, slower first load).

The SPEC.md golden retrieval test sets the pass threshold at ≥80% top-1 accuracy on Spanish corpora — explicitly noting the English model is expected to degrade on Spanish and that the threshold will rise to ≥90% in v0.2 if the multilingual model becomes default.

## Current default

`Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` for v0.1. Multilingual available as a one-line opt-in.

## What needs to be decided

Whether the 5× model size penalty is worth it for the primary dogfood use case. Options:
1. Keep English default, multilingual opt-in (current plan).
2. Flip: multilingual default, English opt-in for size-constrained deploys.
3. Make the model configurable with no default (forces explicit choice at call site).

Decision affects the three-line happy path demo and the golden test threshold.
acceptance_criteria: The embedder-transformers spec records the chosen default model ID and the rationale. The embedder factory default matches.
notes: Decision (2026-04-30): Keep English default — Option 1. Rationale: 5× size penalty (120 MB vs 23 MB) not worth it for v0.1. Multilingual available as one-line opt-in. Threshold stays at ≥80% top-1; rises to ≥90% in v0.2 if we flip.
close_reason: Decided: keep Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 as English default, multilingual as opt-in

title: Decide: tokenizer for markdown chunker — approximation vs embedder's tokenizer
description:
## Why this decision exists

The markdown chunker needs to count tokens to stay within the embedder's context window (typically 256 or 512 tokens for `all-MiniLM-L6-v2`). Two approaches are viable:

- **Own approximation**: character-count heuristic (e.g., chars/4). Fast — no dependency on the embedder being loaded. Slightly inaccurate, may produce over-long chunks that the embedder silently truncates.
- **Embedder's tokenizer**: reuse the tokenizer bundled with transformers.js. Accurate — chunks are guaranteed to fit. Requires the embedder to be loaded before the chunker can run; adds ~5–10 ms per document depending on tokenizer overhead.

## Current default

Own approximation. Chosen for startup speed and chunker/embedder independence.

## What needs to be decided

Whether to keep the approximation for v0.1 or pay the cost of an accurate tokenizer. The spec says to reconsider after measurements. Decision gates the chunker implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A decision is recorded in the chunker-markdown spec with the rationale and any benchmark numbers that informed it. The chunker implementation matches the decision.
close_reason: Confirmed chars/4 approximation for v0.1. Fast, no dependency on embedder being loaded, acceptable inaccuracy for target chunk sizes. Accurate tokenizer (embedder's own) reconsidered after measurements in v0.2.

title: Decide: ingest() scope — markdown-only vs binary file support
description:
## Why this decision exists

The `ingest()` API accepts a `source` (string path/URL) and content. The spec currently scopes v0.1 to markdown-only. The question is whether `ingest()` should also accept `ArrayBuffer | Blob` and handle extraction for PDF/DOCX, or remain strictly markdown-typed.

## Current default

Markdown-only for v0.1. PDF/DOCX extraction deferred as 'a separate library concern.'

## What needs to be decided

Confirm markdown-only is the right boundary for v0.1, or identify whether there's a minimal binary path (e.g., plain-text extraction via FileReader) worth including now. The concern is that relaxing the type signature later may be a breaking change if the chunker seam contract is too narrow.

This is mostly a scope decision — the answer is likely 'keep markdown-only' but it must be recorded so the public-api spec's type signatures are final before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: The public-api spec's ingest() signature is finalized with an explicit statement of what input types are accepted in v0.1 and what the upgrade path is for v0.2+.
close_reason: Confirmed markdown-only for v0.1. ingest() accepts { source: string; text: string; meta?: Record<string, unknown> }. Binary (PDF/DOCX) support deferred to v0.2. Recorded in public-api spec acceptance criteria.

title: Decide: reranker streaming contract — rerank-all-then-yield vs interleave
description:
## Why this decision exists

The spec includes a `queryStream()` method (or implies one via AsyncIterable<Hit>). When a reranker is configured, the top-K results from the index must be reranked before yielding to the caller — but 'before' is ambiguous:

- **Rerank-all-then-yield**: collect all top-K hits, pass the batch to the reranker, yield the reranked list. Latency: full rerank before first hit.
- **Interleave**: yield high-confidence hits immediately, rerank concurrently, adjust order as scores arrive. Lower time-to-first-hit, more complex implementation, potentially confusing order mutations.

## Current default

Rerank-all-then-yield. Noted in SPEC.md as the current default that 'may change after v0.3 ships a reranker.'

## What needs to be decided

Since v0.1 has no reranker (default is `none`), this decision is purely about ensuring the AsyncIterable<Hit> contract doesn't paint v0.3 into a corner. Specifically:
1. Is the current contract compatible with both strategies?
2. Should the seam interface include a hint (e.g., `reranker.supportsStreaming`) to let implementations signal their strategy?

This is a forward-compatibility decision, not a v0.1 implementation task.
acceptance_criteria: The public-api spec's queryStream() contract explicitly states the rerank ordering guarantee and whether it is forward-compatible with interleaved reranking in v0.3. The reranker seam interface is noted as potentially needing a streaming hint.

title: Decide: Worker offload threshold for query() — at what corpus size?
description:
## Why this decision exists

Brute-force flat SIMD cosine search is O(N) over all stored vectors. For small corpora (< 1K chunks) this is imperceptible on the main thread. For the upper bound of v0.1's target (~50K chunks × 384 dims × int8), the math suggests ~20M operations per query — potentially 20–100 ms on a mid-range device, which causes jank.

SPEC.md defers this to v0.3 ('Worker offload threshold — at what corpus size?'). But the decision must be captured now because:
1. The query() API must signal whether it yields back to the event loop between chunks.
2. If Workers are ever added, the API signature must not be incompatible with what ships in v0.1.

## Current default

Main-thread only for v0.1. Worker offload is v0.3 scope.

## What needs to be decided

1. Confirm v0.1 is synchronous on the main thread (wrapped in async for API uniformity).
2. Record the expected measurement that would trigger the v0.3 worker path, so the benchmark gate isn't arbitrary.
3. Confirm the async Promise API in v0.1 is forward-compatible with a Worker-delegated implementation in v0.3 (no API change needed).
acceptance_criteria: The index-flat and public-api specs note the threading model explicitly. A rough corpus-size threshold is documented as the v0.3 trigger. The v0.1 implementation is confirmed forward-compatible.
close_reason: Confirmed main-thread only for v0.1. query() is synchronous internally, wrapped in async Promise for API uniformity. Worker offload is v0.3 scope. The async Promise API is forward-compatible with a Worker-delegated implementation (no signature change needed). Trigger metric: >~50K chunks or measured >50ms query latency on mid-range device.
